GET_CACHE.brief: Macro to access a character's affect cache pointer.
GET_CACHE.details: The GET_CACHE macro retrieves the affect cache associated with a character object, which is stored as a pointer within the character's data structure. It performs a null check on the affect_cache member to determine if the cache has been initialized; if so, it returns the pointer cast to an integer pointer, otherwise it returns nullptr. This macro is frequently used in functions that need to read, update, or display affect cache data, providing a consistent and concise way to access the cache while handling uninitialized cases. Its use ensures safe and uniform access patterns across various affect-related operations within the codebase.
GET_CACHE.notes: The macro assumes that affect_cache is either nullptr or a valid pointer to an integer array. Proper initialization of affect_cache is essential before use to avoid null dereferences.
GET_CACHE.rationale: GET_CACHE exists to streamline access to a character's affect cache, encapsulating the null check and cast operation. This simplifies code that interacts with affect data, reduces boilerplate, and helps prevent errors related to uninitialized caches.
GET_CACHE.name: GET_CACHE

usages.namespaceaffect, void affect::update_cac: Used to retrieve the affect cache pointer for the character, determining whether the cache exists or needs initialization, and to access cache values for updates or checks.
usages.namespaceaffect, String affect::print_ca: Macro invoked to retrieve the affect cache array associated with the character, used to access affect counts by type index.
usages.namespaceaffect, bool affect::in_cache(c: Used to access the cache array of the character to check if a specific affect type is present and has a positive value.

CACHE_SIZE.brief: Represents the maximum number of affect types used for cache sizing and validation.
CACHE_SIZE.details: The CACHE_SIZE macro defines the upper limit for affect type indices by casting the affect::type::tsize constant to an integer. It is utilized across various functions to set the size of affect cache arrays, validate affect type indices, and control iteration over affect caches. By centralizing this value, the macro ensures consistency and simplifies maintenance when the number of affect types changes. Its usage is integral to managing affect-related data structures efficiently and safely.
CACHE_SIZE.notes: This macro is typically used as an array size limit and boundary check in affect cache operations, ensuring indices remain within valid ranges.
CACHE_SIZE.rationale: The macro exists to provide a single, consistent definition of the affect type count, facilitating safe array operations and iteration over affect caches throughout the codebase.
CACHE_SIZE.name: CACHE_SIZE

usages.namespaceaffect, void affect::update_cac: Defines the upper bound for valid affect types and the size of the affect cache array, used for validation and iteration.
usages.namespaceaffect, String affect::print_ca: Defines the upper limit of the loop, used to iterate over all possible affect types in the cache.
usages.namespaceaffect, bool affect::in_cache(c: Serves as an upper bound in the conditional to ensure the type index is within valid cache array bounds.
